title: Detect Sensitive File Read with Falco
description:
## Detect Sensitive File Read with Falco

Falco ships with a built-in rule that alerts whenever a process reads a sensitive file such as `/etc/shadow` — a common indicator of credential harvesting. In this task, you will simulate that attack and capture the resulting Falco alert.

**Prerequisite:** Ensure the **Falco** add-on is installed from the Add-ons catalog (🧩) before starting this task.

**Your task:**

1. Read the file `/etc/shadow` from inside the Pod named `vuln-pod` running in the namespace `falco-lab`. This triggers Falco's built-in rule `Read sensitive file untrusted` (Priority: `Warning`).
2. Query the Falco daemon logs from the `falco` namespace (container `falco` of the Falco DaemonSet).
3. Locate the alert log line containing `shadow` and save it to `/root/falco-alert.log`.

```bash
# Step 1 — trigger the rule:
kubectl exec vuln-pod -n falco-lab -- cat /etc/shadow

# Step 2 — capture the alert:
kubectl logs -n falco daemonset/falco -c falco | grep 'shadow' | tail -n 1 > /root/falco-alert.log

# Step 3 — verify:
cat /root/falco-alert.log
```

hints.title: Triggering the alert
hints.body: Read the sensitive file from inside the container to trigger the Falco rule:

hints.title: Capturing the alert log
hints.body: Search the Falco DaemonSet logs for the alert and redirect it to the target file:
hints.command: kubectl logs -n falco daemonset/falco -c falco | grep 'shadow' | tail -n 1 > /root/falco-alert.log

setup_commands.command: kubectl run vuln-pod --image=alpine:latest -n falco-lab --restart=Never --command -- sleep 3600 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl wait pod vuln-pod -n falco-lab --for=condition=Ready --timeout=60s 2>/dev/null || true

commands.description: Alert log contains a Falco warning about reading a sensitive file
